fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail

seo.title: Florida Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Florida — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license florida, dental assistant certification florida, RDA florida, DANB CDA florida, dental assistant radiography florida

board.name: Florida Board of Dentistry
board.mailing_address: Department of Health, Board of Dentistry, P.O. Box 6330, Tallahassee, FL 32314-6330

license_types.title: Dental Radiographer
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Florida.

steps.title: Understand Expanded Function Requirements
steps.description: Review the Florida Board of Dentistry's statutes and rules to understand the specific expanded functions you wish to perform and the qualifications required, such as formal training or certification.

steps.title: Access MQA Online Services
steps.description: Navigate to the Florida Department of Health's MQA Online Services portal, FLHealthSource.gov, which is the system used for all health-related applications and registrations.

steps.title: Create or Log In to Your Account
steps.description: If you are a first-time applicant, create a new user account on FLHealthSource.gov. Existing users should log in with their credentials.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Select the 'Dental Assistant - Expanded Functions Registration' application. Fill out all required fields accurately and upload any necessary supporting documentation, such as proof of training, course completion, or DAARC certification.

steps.title: Pay the Registration Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $35.00 through the secure online payment system within the FLHealthSource.gov portal.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status on FLHealthSource.gov. Be prepared to respond promptly to any requests for additional information or clarification from the Florida Board of Dentistry.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Dental Radiographer through the Florida Board of Dentistry by registration.

faqs.question: Does Florida require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Florida requires Dental Radiographer through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Florida?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Florida.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Florida?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Florida?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $35. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: Florida does not license general dental assistants but requires registration for dental radiography.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Florida
hero.dek: In Florida, dental assistants are not generally licensed but must register for specific expanded functions through the Florida Department of Health's MQA Online Services portal, FLHealthSource.gov.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Visit FLHealthSource.gov, click 'Apply For A License', select 'Dental Assistant' then 'Expanded Function Registration', create a new account or log in, and complete the online application.
